title: Ceropegia dodsoniana
taxonomy: Ceropegia dodsoniana is classified as a species within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family. Ceropegia dodsoniana is also a member of the genus Ceropegia, which comprises over 100 species of succulent plants with unique, often twisted flowers. The plant is native to Madagascar and is known for its distinctive, star-shaped flowers and long, trailing stems. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Ceropegia dodsoniana is closely related to other plants such as Metaplexis and Hoya.
